reasoning: The Lean statement matches the informal claim exactly. "Nonempty complete metric space X" is encoded as `[MetricSpace X] [CompleteSpace X] [Nonempty X]`. A "sequence of dense open sets" is encoded as `G : ℕ → Set X` with `hG : ∀ n, IsOpen (G n) ∧ Dense (G n)`. The conclusion "∩_1^∞ G_n is not empty" is equivalent to `∃ x, ∀ n, x ∈ G n`, which is the standard unfolding of nonemptiness of a countable intersection. The difference between indexing from 1 vs 0 is immaterial since the claim concerns the intersection of all sets in the sequence and is just a relabeling.
